body:
I am not sure why I removed the concentration here, but not having the concentration in the top fluxes leads to the formation of sea ice in the tropics (radiative transfer leads to a negative balance, which grows sea ice). 

Probably, the only flux that can lead to the formation of new sea ice from a ``no-sea-ice'' condition should be the frazil heat flux. which means that the only way the atmosphere can lead to sea ice formation is through cooling the ocean that freezes

We should probably add the sea ice to the climatology experiment when reworking it as an example
title: Correct net sea ice fluxes

body:
This PR changes how we interpret the `minimum_depth` criterion so that it "opens up" wet/active cells, rather than closing them. I think this is a better criterion, especially since the usual problem with bathymetry is that it tends to close connections rather than open them.

We can consider providing the other option (closing shallow cells) if needed.

cc @NoraLoose

labels.description: When things aren't as smooth as expected
labels.name: bathymetry ⛰️

title: Tweak enforcement of minimum_depth + use kernel
